findings.description: attempt is reset to 0 every loop iteration before the increment, so it never reaches MAX_RETRIES and run_with_retries spins in an infinite loop on any failing job.
subprocess.run with shell=True interpolates untrusted job.payload source/dest into the command string, enabling arbitrary shell command injection.
poll_loop is async but calls blocking time.sleep instead of await asyncio.sleep, freezing the entire event loop and all other coroutines.
Backoff is dead: attempt is 0 at this line (reset on line 69), so RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS*(attempt+1) is always constant 2.0s rather than escalating.
pop(0) on a list is O(n); draining the queue is O(n^2) and should use collections.deque for O(1) dequeue.
sum(values) assumes numeric input; a non-numeric or missing/None value from the JSON payload raises TypeError instead of being validated.
Sleeps after the final attempt before returning False, wasting a full backoff interval on permanently-failing jobs.
source/dest default to empty strings, so a malformed 'convert  ' command runs silently instead of rejecting an incomplete payload.
Bare except Exception swallows all errors including programming bugs, masking root causes and (with the loop bug) never escaping.
poll_loop is declared async but performs no awaits, so the async/asyncio import is misleading dead abstraction.
